era_context: Early Bronze Age. Egypt is transitioning into or is at the threshold of the Early Dynastic Period. Mesopotamia is in the Early Dynastic II–III phase. The Indus Valley Civilisation may be in its early Harappan or pre-Harappan phase, depending on chronology. Writing is emerging in the Near East. The vast majority of human societies are pre-literate.
geographic_coverage_gaps: South Asia outside the Indus Valley
disconfirming_evidence: No events documented for this year. The chronological uncertainty for this period is so large that assigning events to a specific year is not meaningful.
historiographic_note: This year falls in a period of almost complete historical silence. No contemporary written records survive. The only evidence is archaeological: pottery styles, settlement patterns, burial practices, and artefacts. These can be dated using stratigraphic methods and radiocarbon dating, but radiocarbon dating for this period has a margin of error of ±100–200 years, making precision impossible. The historical narrative for this era is constructed from much later sources (king lists, religious texts, chronicles) that are unreliable for chronology and often mythological in content.
